paper_title: Dendrochronological Study at Pirapitinga Ecological Station

methods.description: This method involves selecting and sampling trees for dendrochronological analysis to study growth patterns and environmental interactions.

steps.description: 30 trees of C. langsdorffii were selected from areas of the Mata Seca Sempre-Verde of the PES.
steps.searchable_summary: select 30 C. langsdorffii trees for sampling

steps.description: Two wood radial samples were collected from each tree in the bark-pith direction, with the aid of an increment borer at 1.30m in relation to the soil.
steps.searchable_summary: collect wood samples using increment borer

steps.description: The samples were dried at room temperature and polished with a sanding sequence (between 80 and 600 grit).
All sampled trees were georeferenced and their respective circumferences at breast height (cbh) and height were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: georeference trees and record cbh and height

methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing wood samples for anatomical features and growth ring measurements.

steps.description: The macroscopic anatomical description was carried out according to the IAWA COMITTE classification, with the aid of a stereo microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: perform anatomical description using stereo microscope

steps.description: Identification and marking of growth rings were also carried out by using a stereo microscope.
The samples were scanned in 1200 dpi resolution.
The growth rings width of all series were measured by using the Pro-Plus image analysis software.
steps.searchable_summary: measure growth ring width using Pro-Plus

methods.description: This method involves constructing a chronology from growth ring data and performing statistical analysis to understand growth patterns.

steps.description: Data regarding the rings width were interpreted by the COFECHA statistical program.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret ring width data using COFECHA

steps.description: For the chronology construction, we used the ARSTAN program.
For the correlation of rings formation and meteorological factors, total monthly air temperature and precipitation data from the Earth System Research Laboratory were compiled.
steps.searchable_summary: compile meteorological data for correlation analysis

steps.description: In order to evaluate the relationship between the growth of the copaíba trees over time, simple linear regression was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply simple linear regression to evaluate growth relationship
